---
title: "cositos"
---

**cositos** is a binding-free [anywidget](https://anywidget.dev)-inspired backend core:
define a widget front end once (a small JavaScript module), drive it from **any** Jupyter
kernel language — Python, Julia, Clojure, and more.

New to the vocabulary (widget, comm, ESM, trait…)? Start with the
[**Glossary**](using/glossary.qmd) — every term defined once, in plain language.

## Two doors — pick your path

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
### 🧑‍💻 [Using cositos →](using/index.qmd)
**You author notebooks** and want interactive widgets you can drive from Python, Julia, R,
or Clojure — and save, embed, or publish them.

*Assumes:* you know a notebook language and Jupyter. No widget or JavaScript background
required.

Start: [Quickstart](using/quickstart.qmd) · [Glossary](using/glossary.qmd) ·
[Widget gallery](using/widgets-gallery.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
### 🛠️ [Extending cositos →](extending/index.qmd)
**You build a backend** — porting cositos to a new kernel language, or contributing to the
project internals.

*Assumes:* you know a language ecosystem and are comfortable with protocols and specs.

Start: [Porting guide](extending/porting.md) · [Architecture](extending/architecture.qmd) ·
[Specifications](extending/specs.qmd)
:::

:::

## A live widget, no kernel

This page proves the docs pipeline: the widget below is **rendered statically** — no live
kernel — from a serialized widget document embedded into the page by `cositos.embed`.

```{python}
# | echo: true
# | output: asis
from IPython.display import HTML

from cositos import dump_document, embed_snippet

ESM = """
export default {
  render({ model, el }) {
    const btn = document.createElement("button");
    const out = document.createElement("span");
    const paint = () => { out.textContent = " count = " + model.get("n"); };
    btn.textContent = "increment";
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => { model.set("n", model.get("n") + 1); model.save_changes(); });
    model.on("change:n", paint);
    paint();
    el.appendChild(btn); el.appendChild(out);
  }
}
"""

doc = dump_document([("counter", {"_esm": ESM, "n": 3})])
HTML(embed_snippet(doc))
```

The button is a real cositos widget: its frontend ESM ran in your browser against state
that was serialized in Python and embedded here. Interaction is client-side (no kernel).

## Documentation structure

The site splits by **audience** first, then by [Diátaxis](https://diataxis.fr) content type
within each:

- **[Using cositos](using/index.qmd)** — for notebook authors: tutorials, how-to, API
  reference, and the conceptual guide to keeping widget state consistent.
- **[Extending cositos](extending/index.qmd)** — for backend porters and contributors: the
  porting guide, the normative specifications, the architecture, and contributor setup.
- **[Implementation status](using/status.md)** — what languages and hosts work today.
